title: [DA] Remove "minor algebra" in the RDIV test
author: kasuga-fj
description:
As mentioned in #179653, the "minor algebra" in `testRDIV` is incorrect. This patch deletes that part completely.

Fixes the test case added in #179653.

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-macosx10.6.0"

; Function Attrs: nounwind ssp uwtable
define void @rdiv12(ptr %A, ptr %B) #0 {
entry:
  br label %for.cond1.preheader

for.cond1.preheader:                              ; preds = %for.inc5, %entry
  %B.addr.04 = phi ptr [ %B, %entry ], [ %scevgep, %for.inc5 ]
  %i.03 = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc6, %for.inc5 ]
  br label %for.body3

for.body3:                                        ; preds = %for.body3, %for.cond1.preheader
  %j.02 = phi i64 [ 0, %for.cond1.preheader ], [ %inc, %for.body3 ]
  %B.addr.11 = phi ptr [ %B.addr.04, %for.cond1.preheader ], [ %incdec.ptr, %for.body3 ]
  %conv = trunc i64 %i.03 to i32
  %mul = mul nsw i64 %i.03, 11
  %sub = sub nsw i64 %mul, %j.02
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %sub
  store i32 %conv, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %arrayidx4 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 45
  %0 = load i32, ptr %arrayidx4, align 4
  %incdec.ptr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %B.addr.11, i64 1
  store i32 %0, ptr %B.addr.11, align 4
  %inc = add nsw i64 %j.02, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %inc, 11
  br i1 %exitcond, label %for.body3, label %for.inc5

for.inc5:                                         ; preds = %for.body3
  %scevgep = getelementptr i32, ptr %B.addr.04, i64 11
  %inc6 = add nsw i64 %i.03, 1
  %exitcond5 = icmp ne i64 %inc6, 6
  br i1 %exitcond5, label %for.cond1.preheader, label %for.end7

for.end7:                                         ; preds = %for.inc5
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { nounwind ssp uwtable }

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-macosx10.6.0"

; Function Attrs: nounwind ssp uwtable
define void @rdiv10(ptr %A, ptr %B) #0 {
entry:
  br label %for.cond1.preheader

for.cond1.preheader:                              ; preds = %for.inc5, %entry
  %B.addr.04 = phi ptr [ %B, %entry ], [ %scevgep, %for.inc5 ]
  %i.03 = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc6, %for.inc5 ]
  br label %for.body3

for.body3:                                        ; preds = %for.body3, %for.cond1.preheader
  %j.02 = phi i64 [ 0, %for.cond1.preheader ], [ %inc, %for.body3 ]
  %B.addr.11 = phi ptr [ %B.addr.04, %for.cond1.preheader ], [ %incdec.ptr, %for.body3 ]
  %conv = trunc i64 %i.03 to i32
  %mul = mul nsw i64 %i.03, 11
  %sub = sub nsw i64 %mul, %j.02
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 %sub
  store i32 %conv, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %arrayidx4 = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i64 45
  %0 = load i32, ptr %arrayidx4, align 4
  %incdec.ptr = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %B.addr.11, i64 1
  store i32 %0, ptr %B.addr.11, align 4
  %inc = add nsw i64 %j.02, 1
  %exitcond = icmp ne i64 %inc, 10
  br i1 %exitcond, label %for.body3, label %for.inc5

for.inc5:                                         ; preds = %for.body3
  %scevgep = getelementptr i32, ptr %B.addr.04, i64 10
  %inc6 = add nsw i64 %i.03, 1
  %exitcond5 = icmp ne i64 %inc6, 6
  br i1 %exitcond5, label %for.cond1.preheader, label %for.end7

for.end7:                                         ; preds = %for.inc5
  ret void
}

attributes #0 = { nounwind ssp uwtable }

define void @f(ptr %A) {
entry:
  br label %loop.i.header

loop.i.header:                                    ; preds = %loop.i.latch, %entry
  %i = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.inc, %loop.i.latch ]
  %i.mul = mul i64 %i, 9223372036854775807
  %gep.0 = getelementptr i8, ptr %A, i64 -2
  store i8 0, ptr %gep.0, align 1
  br label %loop.j.header

loop.j.header:                                    ; preds = %loop.j.latch, %loop.i.header
  %j = phi i64 [ 0, %loop.i.header ], [ %j.inc, %loop.j.latch ]
  %j.mul = mul i64 %j, 9223372036854775807
  %offset.tmp = add i64 %i.mul, %j.mul
  %offset = add i64 %offset.tmp, -2
  %cond = icmp eq i64 %i, %j
  br i1 %cond, label %if.then, label %loop.j.latch

if.then:                                          ; preds = %loop.j.header
  %gep.1 = getelementptr i8, ptr %A, i64 %offset
  store i8 1, ptr %gep.1, align 1
  br label %loop.j.latch

loop.j.latch:                                     ; preds = %if.then, %loop.j.header
  %j.inc = add i64 %j, 1
  %ec.j = icmp eq i64 %j.inc, 2
  br i1 %ec.j, label %loop.i.latch, label %loop.j.header

loop.i.latch:                                     ; preds = %loop.j.latch
  %i.inc = add i64 %i, 1
  %ec.i = icmp eq i64 %i.inc, 2
  br i1 %ec.i, label %exit, label %loop.i.header

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop.i.latch
  ret void
}

comments.author: kasuga-fj
comments.body:
* **#179654** 👈 (View in Graphite) * **#179653** * `main`

This stack of pull requests is managed by Graphite. Learn more about stacking.

comments.author: sjoerdmeijer
comments.body: I am fine with this and this looks good to me, but just one question: do you think is there a way this can be salvaged and made correct? In other words, my question here is if it is worth leaving a TODO in comments explaining what the opportunity is here.  Maybe leaving such a note is the easiest in case someone wants to pick this up later.

comments.author: kasuga-fj
comments.body:
> do you think is there a way this can be salvaged and made correct? In other words, my question here is if it is worth leaving a TODO in comments explaining what the opportunity is here.

I've thought about something similar once, but I think it's difficult to make it sound. For some dependence tests, what nested addrecs like `{{c,+,b},+,a}` (i.e., `[a*i + b*j + c]`) implies is unclear, and probably have the same problem as https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/176367#issuecomment-3849431540. That is, to correct this, we may need to do something similar to what we need to fix the MIV tests. If that's the case, I think it's better to leave nested addrecs to the MIV tests rather than trying to handle them by the RDIV tests.

comments.author: amehsan
comments.body:
> @amehsan LLVM has always removed abandoned/dead/non-working/bit-rotten code from the repository. Please follow the community practices when reviewing.

I thought we are in the process of fixing DA tests, and we are going through them one by one. If at some point this process stops it makes more sense to remove anything not-yet-fixed at that point. (Otherwise we should remove almost all the DA tests today).

But, this is not that important. If you prefer to remove it I am fine with it.

comments.author: kasuga-fj
comments.body:
> (Otherwise we should remove almost all the DA tests today).

FWIW, my preference is to remove all tests once from DA. I believe the current direction represents a middle ground that we have agreed on (not remove entirely, but delete bit-rotten part aggressively). I'd appreciate it if you could keep in mind that there have been various discussions leading up to this point.
